sentences.chinese: 歲餘，其友人安定太守西河孫會宗，知略士也，與惲書諫戒之，為言大臣廢退，當闔門惶懼，為可憐之意，不當治產業，通賓客，有稱譽。
sentences.literal: After more than a year his friend Sun Huizong, Guardian of Anding from Xihe, a man of strategy and insight, wrote Yun a letter of remonstrance and warning, saying that when great ministers are dismissed and retired they ought to shut their gates in fear and trembling, showing an attitude fit to win pity, and ought not to manage estates, traffic with guests, or invite praise.
sentences.idiomatic: A year later his friend Sun Huizong, governor of Anding, a shrewd man from Xihe, wrote urging him to live like a disgraced minister—bar the gates, look wretched, beg sympathy—and not to rebuild a fortune, throw open his house to clients, or court a reputation.

sentences.chinese: 惲宰相子，少顯朝廷，一朝以暗昧語言見廢，內懷不服，報會宗書曰：
sentences.literal: Yun was a chancellor's son who had shown himself early at court; in one morning, on account of obscure speech, he was cast aside; inwardly he did not submit, and he replied to Huizong's letter, saying:
sentences.idiomatic: Yun was a prime minister's son who had glittered at court while young; one murky phrase had ruined him, and resentment still seethed as he answered Sun Huizong:

sentences.chinese: 惲材朽行穢，文質無所底，幸賴先人余業得備宿衛，遭遇時變以獲爵位，終非其任，卒與禍會。
sentences.literal: Yun's timber is rotten and his conduct foul; in pattern and substance he has reached no bottom; fortunately relying on his forebears' remaining estate he was able to serve in night guard, met with the changes of the times and thereby obtained rank and title, but in the end it was not his to bear, and he ended meeting calamity.
sentences.idiomatic: I am warped wood and foul conduct, neither learned nor presentable; I inherited a name that bought me a slot in the palace guard, then luck and turmoil raised me to nobility—posts I was never fit for, and disaster followed.

sentences.chinese: 足下哀其愚，蒙賜書，教督以所不及，殷勤甚厚。
sentences.literal: You pity my stupidity, have favoured me with a letter, instructing and overseeing what I lack—your earnest kindness is very great.
sentences.idiomatic: You pity my folly and lecture me where I fall short—kindness I do not forget.

sentences.chinese: 然竊恨足下不深惟其終始，而猥隨俗之毀譽也。
sentences.literal: Yet I privately resent that you have not deeply pondered the beginning and end, but vulgarly follow the world's praise and blame.
sentences.idiomatic: Yet I resent that you never traced my story from start to finish but parrot the world's gossip.

sentences.chinese: 言鄙陋之愚心，若逆指而文過，默而息乎，恐違孔氏「各言爾志」之義，故敢略陳其愚，唯君子察焉！
sentences.literal: If I speak my base and shallow foolish heart, it is as if I oppose your finger and gloss my fault; if I am silent and rest, I fear I violate Confucius's meaning of 'each declare your intent'—therefore I dare briefly state my folly; may the gentleman examine it!
sentences.idiomatic: If I hide my thoughts I twist your meaning; if I hold my tongue I break Confucius's rule that each man should speak his mind—so I lay my folly before you; judge it as you will.

sentences.chinese: 惲家方隆盛時，乘硃輪者十人，位在列卿，爵為通侯，總領從官，與聞政事，曾不能以此時有所建明，以宣德化，又不能與群僚同心並力，陪輔朝廷之遺忘，已負竊位素餐之責久矣。
sentences.literal: When Yun's family was at its height of splendour, those who rode vermillion-wheeled carriages were ten in number; his position stood among the ranked ministers, his noble rank was full marquis, he headed the attendant officials and heard government affairs—yet he could not at that time achieve anything to make clear virtue and transformation, nor could he join his colleagues in one heart and combined strength, assisting the court in what it overlooked—already for long he bore the blame of stealing office and eating salary empty.
sentences.idiomatic: When my house flourished, ten kinsmen rode the vermilion-wheeled carriages of the highest nobility; I myself ranked among the nine ministers, bore a full marquisate, commanded the emperor's bodyguard, and sat in on policy—yet I never once spoke a word that might spread the court's virtue, never rallied my peers to patch what the throne forgot. Long before my fall I deserved the charge of drawing pay for an empty chair.

sentences.chinese: 懷祿貪勢，不能自退，遭遇變故，橫被口語，身幽北闕，妻子滿獄。
sentences.literal: He embraced salary and coveted power, could not retire of himself, met with sudden change, was smeared by loose talk, his person was confined at the northern gate tower, wife and children filled the prisons.
sentences.idiomatic: I clung to office and salary, would not step down, then a storm of slander locked me in the north gate prison while my wife and children filled the cells.

sentences.chinese: 當此之時，自以夷滅不足以塞責，豈意得全首領，復奉先人之丘墓乎？
sentences.literal: At that time he thought even extermination would not suffice to fill his responsibility—how could he have expected to keep his head whole and again tend his forebears' tombs?
sentences.idiomatic: I was sure clan execution would not atone for my guilt—who dreamed I would keep my head and walk my father's hill again?

sentences.chinese: 伏惟聖主之恩，不可勝量。
sentences.literal: Prostrate, he reflected that the sage ruler's grace cannot be measured.
sentences.idiomatic: I bow to a grace no man can measure.

sentences.chinese: 君子游道，樂以忘憂；
sentences.literal: The gentleman who roams the Way finds joy and forgets sorrow.
sentences.idiomatic: The gentleman who walks the Way finds joy and forgets his cares.

sentences.chinese: 小人全軀，說以忘罪。
sentences.literal: The petty man who preserves his body finds pleasure and forgets guilt.
sentences.idiomatic: The small man who saves his skin finds comfort and forgets his shame.

sentences.chinese: 竊自思念，過已大矣，行已虧矣，長為農夫以沒世矣。
sentences.literal: Privately he thought: my fault is already great, my conduct already impaired; I shall long be a farmer to the end of my days.
sentences.idiomatic: I told myself my crimes were grave and my name ruined—that I would die a ploughman.

sentences.chinese: 是故身率妻子，戮力耕桑，灌園治產，以給公上，不意當復用此為譏議也。
sentences.literal: Therefore he personally led wife and children, exerted strength in ploughing and sericulture, irrigated the garden and managed production, in order to supply the public treasury—he did not expect again to be faulted on this account.
sentences.idiomatic: So I took my family to the fields, hoed and mulched, watered the garden, and sent the tax grain to the granary—I never thought that life would earn me fresh slander.

sentences.chinese: 夫人情所不能止者，聖人弗禁，故君父至尊親，送其終也，有時而既。
sentences.literal: What human feeling cannot stop, the sage does not forbid; therefore though lord and father are supremely venerated and intimate, in sending them off to the end there is a time when it is finished.
sentences.idiomatic: What passion cannot restrain, even sages do not forbid: lord and father are the highest bonds, yet mourning for them also has its term.

sentences.chinese: 臣之得罪，已三年矣。
sentences.literal: Your servant's offense is already three years old.
sentences.idiomatic: It is three years since my conviction.

sentences.chinese: 田家作苦，歲時伏臘，亨羊□羔，鬥酒自勞。
sentences.literal: The farmer's toil is bitter; at the seasonal offerings of summer and winter rest he boils sheep and [roasts] lamb, and with a flagon of wine refreshes himself.
sentences.idiomatic: Farm work is brutal; on the summer and winter festival days I boil mutton, roast lamb, and toast myself with a jar of wine.

sentences.chinese: 家本秦也，能為秦聲。
sentences.literal: The family's origin was Qin; they could perform Qin songs.
sentences.idiomatic: We are Qin folk by stock and still sing in the Qin mode.

sentences.chinese: 婦，趙女也，雅善鼓瑟。
sentences.literal: His wife was a Zhao woman, skilled at strumming the se.
sentences.idiomatic: My wife is a Zhao girl who plucks the se better than I deserve.

sentences.chinese: 奴婢歌者數人，酒後耳熱，仰天拊缶而呼烏烏。
sentences.literal: Several slave-girl singers; after wine, ears hot, he faced heaven, beat the earthen jar, and cried 'wu wu.'
sentences.idiomatic: A few bondmaids carry the tune; when the wine fires my blood I beat the clay fou and howl the old Qin refrains.

sentences.chinese: 其詩曰：「田彼南山，蕪穢不治，種一頃豆，落而為其。
sentences.literal: The poem says: 'I farm that southern hill; weeds choke it untended; I sowed a hundred qing of beans—they fell and turned to beanstalks.
sentences.idiomatic: My impromptu lines run: 'South Mountain lies untilled, rank with weeds I never cleared; I sowed a full qing of beans—only beanstalks dropped where the pods should be.

sentences.chinese: 人生行樂耳，須富貴何時！」
sentences.literal: "Man's life is for pleasure—when will wealth and honour come!"
sentences.idiomatic: "Life is for the living—wait for fortune and you wait forever!"

sentences.chinese: 是日也，拂衣而喜，奮袖低卬，頓足起舞，誠淫荒無度，不知其不可也。
sentences.literal: That day, brushing his robes in joy, waving sleeves high and low, stamping his feet and rising to dance—truly he was dissolute beyond measure and did not know it was impermissible.
sentences.idiomatic: That day I shook out my sleeves, stamped the beat, and danced like a fool—shameless, and blind to how it looked.

sentences.chinese: 惲幸有餘祿，方糴賤販貴，逐什一之利，此賈豎之事，污辱之處，惲親行之。
sentences.literal: Yun fortunately had surplus salary; he was then buying cheap and selling dear, pursuing a one-in-ten profit—this is peddlers' work, a place of filth and insult, yet Yun personally did it.
sentences.idiomatic: I still had a trickle of stipend, so I bought low and sold high for the merchant's ten-percent—work fit for a stall boy, yet I did it with my own hands.

sentences.chinese: 下流之人，眾毀所歸，不寒而慄。
sentences.literal: A man of the lower current is where the crowd's slander gathers; he shivers though it is not cold.
sentences.idiomatic: Stand in the mud downstream and every slur finds you—you shiver without a wind.

sentences.chinese: 雖雅知惲者，猶隨風而靡，尚何稱譽之有！
sentences.literal: Even those who know Yun well still follow the wind and bend—what praise is there still to speak of!
sentences.idiomatic: Even old friends bend with the rumor mill—what praise could reach me now?

sentences.chinese: 董生不雲乎？
sentences.literal: Did not Master Dong say?
sentences.idiomatic: Has not Master Dong written?

sentences.chinese: 「明明求仁義，常恐不能化民者，卿大夫意也；
sentences.literal: 'Those who plainly seek benevolence and right, ever fearing they cannot transform the people—that is the intent of ministers and grandees;
sentences.idiomatic: "Those who burn to spread benevolence and dread lest the people never mend—that is the temper of high ministers;

sentences.chinese: 明明求財利，常恐困乏者，庶人之事也。」
sentences.literal: those who plainly seek wealth and profit, ever fearing want—that is the business of commoners.'"
sentences.idiomatic: those who plainly chase coin and fear poverty—that is the commoner's trade."

sentences.chinese: 故「道不同，不相為謀。」
sentences.literal: Therefore 'when the Way differs, they do not counsel one another.'"
sentences.idiomatic: Hence the saying: different roads, no shared counsel."

sentences.chinese: 今子尚安得以卿大夫之制而責僕哉！
sentences.literal: Now how can you, sir, still apply the standards of ministers and grandees to blame your servant!
sentences.idiomatic: How dare you measure a farmer by a minister's yardstick?

sentences.chinese: 夫西河魏土，文侯所興，有段干木、田子方之遺風，漂然皆有節概，知去就之分。
sentences.literal: The land west of the river was Wei territory, where Marquis Wen flourished; it retains the surviving airs of Duan Ganmu and Tian Zifang—drifting, all have integrity and mettle, knowing the division between going and staying.
sentences.idiomatic: Your own Xihe was the heart of Wei, raised by Marquess Wen and still breathing the austerity of Duan Ganmu and Tian Zifang—men who knew when to serve and when to walk away.

sentences.chinese: 頃者，足下離舊土，臨安定，安定山谷之間，昆戎舊壤，子弟貪鄙，豈習俗之移人哉？
sentences.literal: Recently you, sir, left your old soil and came to Anding; between the mountains and valleys of Anding lies the old ground of the Kun Rong—the sons and younger brothers are greedy and base—is it that custom moves men?
sentences.idiomatic: You have moved to Anding, a gully land of Rong stock where the young men are coarse and grasping—has the border curdled your taste, or only revealed it?

sentences.chinese: 於今乃睹子之志矣。
sentences.literal: Only now do I see your intent.
sentences.idiomatic: Now I see what you are really after.

sentences.chinese: 方當盛漢之隆，願勉旃，毋多談。
sentences.literal: Just as mighty Han is at its height, I hope you will strive—do not speak overmuch.
sentences.idiomatic: Han still rides its zenith—spare me further sermons.

sentences.chinese: 又惲兄子安平侯譚為典屬國，謂惲曰：「西河太守建平杜侯前以罪過出，今徵為御史大夫。
sentences.literal: Moreover Yun's elder brother's son, Marquis of Anping Tan, was Commissioner for Dependent States; he said to Yun, "The Guardian of Xihe, Marquis Jianping Du, formerly went out on account of crime, but now has been summoned to be Imperial Counselor.
sentences.idiomatic: His nephew Tan, marquis of Anping and commissioner for dependent states, added gossip: "Du, the Jianping marquis who was governor of Xihe, left office under a cloud but is back as imperial counselor.

sentences.chinese: 侯罪薄，又有功，且復用。」
sentences.literal: The marquis's crime was light and he also had merit; he will be employed again."
sentences.idiomatic: His guilt was slight and his service real—he will rise again."

sentences.chinese: 惲曰：「有功何益？
sentences.literal: Yun said, "What good is merit?
sentences.idiomatic: Yun answered, "Merit buys nothing.

sentences.chinese: 縣官不足為盡力。」
sentences.literal: The Son of Heaven is not worth exhausting oneself for."
sentences.idiomatic: This emperor is not worth breaking yourself for."

sentences.chinese: 惲素與蓋寬饒、韓延壽善，譚即曰：「縣官實然，蓋司隸、韓馮翊皆盡力吏也，俱坐事誅。」
sentences.literal: Yun had always been on good terms with Gai Kuanrao and Han Yanshou; Tan immediately said, "The Son of Heaven is indeed so—Metropolitan Superintendent Gai and Governor Han of Zuo Fengyi were both diligent officials, yet both sat on affairs and were executed."
sentences.idiomatic: Yun had been close to Gai Kuanrao and Han Yanshou; Tan shot back, "If that is how the court treats men, Gai the metropolitan superintendent and Han the governor of Zuo Fengyi gave everything they had—and both died on the block."

sentences.chinese: 會有日食變，騶馬猥佐成上書告惲「驕奢不悔過，日食之咎，此人所致。」
sentences.literal: It happened that there was a solar-eclipse portent; the groom-attendant Cheng memorialized accusing Yun of 'arrogance and luxury without repenting; the blame for the eclipse lies with this man.'
sentences.idiomatic: Then came an eclipse; a stable-hand named Cheng memorialized that Yun's swaggering refusal to reform had brought down heaven's rebuke.

sentences.chinese: 章下廷尉案驗，得所予會宗書，宣帝見而惡之。
sentences.literal: The memorial was sent down; the Commandant of Justice investigated and obtained the letter given to Huizong; Emperor Xuandi saw it and detested it.
sentences.idiomatic: The throne forwarded the charge; the commandant seized the letter to Sun Huizong, and Xuandi read it with disgust.

sentences.chinese: 廷尉當惲大逆無道，要斬。
sentences.literal: The Commandant of Justice judged Yun guilty of great treason and impiety; he was cut in two at the waist.
sentences.idiomatic: The commandant ruled capital treason; Yun was halved at the waist.

sentences.chinese: 妻子徙酒泉郡。
sentences.literal: Wife and children were banished to Jiuquan commandery.
sentences.idiomatic: His wife and children were marched to Jiuquan.

sentences.chinese: 譚坐不諫正惲，與相應，有怨望語，免為庶人。
sentences.literal: Tan sat for failing to remonstrate with and correct Yun and for answering him with resentful words; he was dismissed to commoner status.
sentences.idiomatic: Tan fell for not rebuking his uncle and for answering him with mutinous talk—stripped to commoner.

sentences.chinese: 召拜成為郎，諸在位與惲厚善者，未央衛尉韋玄成、京兆尹張敞及孫會宗等，皆免官。
sentences.literal: Cheng was summoned and appointed gentleman; all those in office who had been thick with Yun—Commandant of the Palace Guard Wei Xuancheng, Governor of the Capital Zhang Chang, and Sun Huizong among others—were all dismissed from office.
sentences.idiomatic: Cheng was rewarded with a gentleman cadetship; every official who had stood close to Yun—Wei Xuancheng of the palace guard, Zhang Chang of the capital, Sun Huizong—lost his post.

sentences.chinese: 蔡義，河內溫人也。
sentences.literal: Cai Yi was a man of Wen in Henei.
sentences.idiomatic: Cai Yi came from Wen county in Henei.

sentences.chinese: 以明經給事大將軍莫府。
sentences.literal: By clarity in the classics he served in the Grand General's headquarters.
sentences.idiomatic: His classical learning won him a clerkship in Huo Guang's headquarters.

sentences.chinese: 家貧，常步行，資禮不逮眾門下，好事者相合為義買犢車，令乘之。
sentences.literal: His family was poor; he always walked; his resources for ritual did not match the many retainers—men who loved a good deed clubbed together to buy Yi a calf-drawn cart and had him ride in it.
sentences.idiomatic: He was too poor to keep pace on foot with richer clients, so well-wishers pooled cash and bought him a ox-drawn gig.

sentences.chinese: 數歲，遷補覆盎城門候。
sentences.literal: After several years he was transferred and appointed gatekeeper of Fugang Gate.
sentences.idiomatic: Within a few years he was posted warden of Fugang Gate.

sentences.chinese: 久之，詔求能為《韓詩》者，征義待詔，久不進見。
sentences.literal: After a long while an edict sought one who could expound the Han version of the Odes; Yi was summoned as awaiting orders, but for long was not advanced to audience.
sentences.idiomatic: An edict called for an expert on the Han Odes; Yi answered as a reserve scholar but languished without an audience.

sentences.chinese: 義上疏曰：「臣山東草萊之人，行能亡所比，容貌不及眾，然而不棄人倫者，竊以聞道於先師，自托於經術也。
sentences.literal: Yi submitted a memorial saying, "Your servant is a man of the grass and brush east of the mountains; in conduct and ability there is none to compare, in looks he does not match the crowd—yet he is not cast out from human relations because he has privately heard the Way from former teachers and entrusted himself to classical learning.
sentences.idiomatic: He filed a memorial: "I am a weed scholar from east of the mountains—no beauty, no genius—yet I cling to human society because old masters taught me the classics."

sentences.chinese: 願賜清閒之燕，得盡精思於前。」
sentences.literal: I beg to be granted a clear and quiet banquet, that I may exhaust refined thought in your presence."
sentences.idiomatic: Grant me one quiet audience and I will lay out every thought I have."

sentences.chinese: 上召見義，說《詩》，甚說之，擢為光祿大夫給事中，進授昭帝。
sentences.literal: The emperor summoned Yi for audience; he discoursed on the Odes; the emperor was very pleased, promoted him to Grand Palace Grandee and Palace Attendant, and advanced to instruct Emperor Zhao.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor received him, loved his lecture on the Odes, named him grand palace grandee with palace access, and set him to tutor young Emperor Zhao.

sentences.chinese: 數歲，拜為少府，遷御史大夫，代楊敝為丞相，封陽平侯。
sentences.literal: After several years he was appointed Junior Minister, promoted to Imperial Counselor, succeeded Yang Chang as chancellor, and was enfeoffed Marquis of Yangping.
sentences.idiomatic: Within a few years he rose to junior minister, then imperial counselor, then replaced Yang Chang as chancellor with the marquisate of Yangping.

sentences.chinese: 又以定策安宗廟益封，加賜黃金二百斤。
sentences.literal: Again for fixing the succession and securing the ancestral temples his enfeoffment was increased, with an additional gift of two hundred catties of gold.
sentences.idiomatic: He earned a larger fief and two hundred catties of gold for helping settle the imperial succession.

sentences.chinese: 義為丞相時年八十餘，短小無鬚眉，貌似老嫗，行步俯僂，常兩吏扶夾乃能行。
sentences.literal: When Yi was chancellor he was more than eighty years old, short in stature, without whiskers or eyebrows, his appearance like an old woman, his gait stooped; constantly two clerks had to support him on either side before he could walk.
sentences.idiomatic: As chancellor he was past eighty, beardless, wizened as a crone, so stooped that two clerks had to prop him under the arms.

sentences.chinese: 時大將軍光秉政，議者或言光置宰相不選賢，苟用可專制者。
sentences.literal: At that time Grand General Huo Guang held power; some critics said Guang in setting up the chancellor did not choose the worthy but picked whoever could be dominated.
sentences.idiomatic: Critics muttered that Huo Guang picked a chancellor he could puppeteer.

sentences.chinese: 光聞之，謂侍中左右及官屬曰：「以為人主師當為宰相，何謂云云？
sentences.literal: When Guang heard it he said to the palace attendants left and right and his office staff, "To be the ruler's teacher one ought to be chancellor—what is this talk?
sentences.idiomatic: Huo Guang heard and told his staff, "A tutor to the Son of Heaven should wear the chancellor's seal—what idle chatter is this?

sentences.chinese: 此語不可使天下聞也。」
sentences.literal: This language must not be allowed to spread through all under Heaven."
sentences.idiomatic: Let that rumor go no further."

sentences.chinese: 義為相四歲，薨，謚曰節侯。
sentences.literal: Yi was chancellor four years, then died; his posthumous title was Marquis Jie.
sentences.idiomatic: He died after four years in office and was canonized Jie, "the Prudent."

sentences.chinese: 無子，國除。
sentences.literal: He had no son; the state was abolished.
sentences.idiomatic: He left no heir; the marquisate lapsed.

sentences.chinese: 陳萬年
sentences.literal: Chen Wannian.
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Wannian

sentences.chinese: 陳萬年字幼公，沛郡相人也。
sentences.literal: Chen Wannian, courtesy name Yougong, was a man of Xiang in Pei commandery.
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Wannian, courtesy Yougong, was a native of Xiang in Pei.

sentences.chinese: 為郡吏，察舉，至縣令，遷廣陵太守，以高弟入為右扶風，遷太僕。
sentences.literal: He was a commandery clerk, recommended and promoted to county magistrate, transferred Guardian of Guangling, entered as Youfufeng with highest rank, and promoted Grand Coachman.
sentences.idiomatic: He rose from county clerk through recommendation to magistrate, then governor of Guangling, then entered the capital as Youfufeng with top marks, then grand coachman.

sentences.chinese: 萬年廉平，內行修，然善事人。
sentences.literal: Wannian was incorrupt and fair, his inner conduct cultivated, yet he was skilled at serving men.
sentences.idiomatic: He was honest and cultivated at home, yet a master flatterer in public.

sentences.chinese: 賂遺外戚許、史，傾家自盡，尤事樂陵侯史高。
sentences.literal: He bribed the maternal kin Xu and Shi, exhausting his household to the utmost, and especially served Marquis of Leling Shi Gao.
sentences.idiomatic: He poured his fortune into gifts for the Xu and Shi consort clans and fawned especially on Marquis of Leling Shi Gao.

sentences.chinese: 丞相丙吉病，中二千石上謁問疾。
sentences.literal: Chancellor Bing Ji fell ill; those of full two thousand piculs and above called to inquire after his illness.
sentences.idiomatic: When Chancellor Bing Ji fell ill, every minister at two thousand piculs paid a sick call.

sentences.chinese: 遣家丞出謝，謝已皆去，萬年獨留，昏夜乃歸。
sentences.literal: He sent out his household steward to thank them; after the thanks all departed, but Wannian alone remained and did not return until deep night.
sentences.idiomatic: The steward bowed them out—Wannian alone lingered until midnight.

sentences.chinese: 及吉病甚，上自臨，問以大臣行能。
sentences.literal: When Ji was very gravely ill the emperor himself came to visit and asked about the conduct and ability of the great ministers.
sentences.idiomatic: When Ji neared death the emperor came in person and asked which ministers were fit to succeed.

sentences.chinese: 吉薦於定國、杜延年及萬年，萬年竟代定國為御史大夫八歲，病卒。
sentences.literal: Ji recommended Yu Dinggu, Du Yannian, and Wannian; Wannian in the end replaced Dinggu as Imperial Counselor for eight years, then died of illness.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji named Yu Dinggu, Du Yannian, and Wannian; Wannian eventually succeeded Yu Dinggu as imperial counselor for eight years until he died in office.

sentences.chinese: 子咸字子康，年十八，以萬年任為郎。
sentences.literal: His son Xian, courtesy name Zikang, at age eighteen through Wannian's appointment became a gentleman.
sentences.idiomatic: His son Chen Xian, courtesy Zikang, entered as a gentleman cadet at eighteen through his father's rank.

sentences.chinese: 有異材，抗直，數言事，刺譏近臣，書數十上，遷為左曹。
sentences.literal: He had unusual talent, was upright and blunt, repeatedly spoke on affairs, satirizing the near ministers; his memorials numbered in the tens, and he was promoted to Left Assistant.
sentences.idiomatic: He had rare ability, spoke bluntly, peppered the throne with memorials mocking the inner circle, and won promotion to left aide.

sentences.chinese: 萬年嘗病，召咸教戒於床下，語至夜半，咸睡，頭觸屏風。
sentences.literal: Wannian once fell ill and summoned Xian to instruct and warn him at the foot of the bed; he spoke until midnight; Xian slept, his head striking the screen.
sentences.idiomatic: Once, ill in bed, Wannian lectured him past midnight until Xian nodded off and cracked his head on the screen.

sentences.chinese: 萬年大怒，欲仗之，曰：「乃公教戒汝，汝反睡，不聽吾言，何也？」
sentences.literal: Wannian was greatly angry and wished to beat him with a staff, saying, "Your old man instructs and warns you, yet you turn around and sleep, not listening to my words—why?"
sentences.idiomatic: Wannian roared that he would cane him: "Your father is handing you the keys to survival and you snore?"

sentences.chinese: 咸叩頭謝曰：「具曉所言，大要教咸諂也。」
sentences.literal: Xian knocked his head in apology and said, "I fully understand what was said; the main gist was teaching Xian to flatter."
sentences.idiomatic: Xian kowtowed: "I heard every word—the lesson was sycophancy."

sentences.chinese: 萬年乃不復言。
sentences.literal: Wannian then spoke no more.
sentences.idiomatic: Wannian never finished the lecture.

sentences.chinese: 萬年死後，元帝擢咸為御史中丞，總領州郡奏事，課第諸刺史，內執法殿中，公卿以下皆敬憚之。
sentences.literal: After Wannian died Emperor Yuan promoted Xian to Palace Secretary to the Imperial Counselor, heading memorials from provinces and commanderies, ranking the governors, enforcing law within the palace—down to the dukes all feared and respected him.
sentences.idiomatic: After his father's death Yuan appointed him palace secretary to the censorate, sorting provincial memorials, grading governors, and policing the inner court—every minister walked softly around him.

sentences.chinese: 是時，中書令石顯用事顓權，咸頗言顯短，顯等恨之。
sentences.literal: At that time Palace Secretary Shi Xian held power and monopolized authority; Chen Xian often spoke of Shi Xian's shortcomings; Shi Xian and his faction hated Chen Xian.
sentences.idiomatic: Palace secretary Shi Xian monopolized power; Chen Xian exposed his faults and earned a bitter enemy.

sentences.chinese: 時槐裡令硃雲殘酷殺不辜，有司舉奏，未下。
sentences.literal: At that time the magistrate of Huaili, Zhu Yun, was cruel and killed the innocent; the authorities had raised a memorial, but it had not yet been approved.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun, magistrate of Huaili, had tortured innocents to death; a bill of impeachment sat unsigned on the desk.

sentences.chinese: 咸素善雲，雲從刺候，教令上書自訟。
sentences.literal: Xian had always been on good terms with Yun; Yun had him watched and instructed to memorialize in self-defence.
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Xian, Zhu's friend, tipped him to file a counter-memorial.

sentences.chinese: 於是石顯微伺知之，白奏咸漏洩省中語，下獄掠治，減死，髡為城旦，因廢。
sentences.literal: Thereupon Shi Xian secretly spied and learned of it, reported in a memorial that Xian had leaked language from within the palace, cast him into prison and flogged him in investigation; his death sentence was commuted, he was shaved for wall corvée, and thereby ruined.
sentences.idiomatic: Shi Xian's agents caught the leak, denounced Chen Xian for betraying palace secrets, had him flogged in jail, spared his life but shaved him for wall labor, and ended his career.

sentences.chinese: 時，車騎將軍王音輔政，信用陳湯。
sentences.literal: At that time General of Chariots and Cavalry Wang Yin assisted in government and trusted Chen Tang.
sentences.idiomatic: Later General of Chariots and Cavalry Wang Yin governed and favored Chen Tang.

sentences.chinese: 咸數賂遺湯，予書曰：「即蒙子公力，得入帝城，死不恨。」
sentences.literal: Xian repeatedly bribed Tang and sent a letter saying, "If I rely on your, Zigong's, power to enter the imperial city, I will die without regret."
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Xian showered Tang with gifts and wrote, "If you, Zigong, pull me back inside the walls, I can die content."

sentences.chinese: 後竟征入為少府。
sentences.literal: Later he was indeed summoned in as Junior Minister.
sentences.idiomatic: Tang did bring him back as junior minister.

sentences.chinese: 少府多寶物、屬官，咸皆鉤校，發其奸臧，沒入辜榷財物。
sentences.literal: The Junior Minister's office had many treasures and subordinate offices; Xian audited them all, exposed their crafty corruption, and confiscated goods seized under the monopoly statutes.
sentences.idiomatic: He inventoried the ministry's vaults and bureaus, exposed every embezzlement, and impounded the hoarded monopoly profits.

sentences.chinese: 官屬及諸中宮黃門、鉤盾、掖庭官吏，舉奏按論，畏咸，皆失氣。
sentences.literal: Subordinate offices and all the inner-palace yellow-gate attendants, hook-shield and rear-palace clerks—he raised memorials to investigate and sentence them; they feared Xian and all lost their breath.
sentences.idiomatic: Yellow gates, hook-shield guards, harem clerks—he impeached them in batches until they went pale at his name.

sentences.chinese: 為少府三歲，與翟方進有隙。
sentences.literal: As Junior Minister for three years he had a rift with Zhai Fangjin.
sentences.idiomatic: After three years he quarreled with Zhai Fangjin.

sentences.chinese: 方進為丞相，奏：「咸前為郡守，所在殘酷，毒螫加於吏民。
sentences.literal: Fangjin as chancellor memorialized: "Xian formerly as guardian was cruel where he ruled, venom stinging officials and people.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhai, now chancellor, charged that Chen Xian had been a savage governor who poisoned every place he ruled.

sentences.chinese: 主守盜，受所監。
sentences.literal: He guarded and stole, accepting bribes from those he supervised.
sentences.idiomatic: He stole from the treasuries he guarded and took bribes from his own staff.

sentences.chinese: 而官媚邪臣陳湯以求薦舉。
sentences.literal: Yet as an official he fawned on the evil minister Chen Tang to seek recommendation.
sentences.idiomatic: He debased himself flattering Chen Tang for a recommendation.

sentences.chinese: 苟得無恥，不宜處位。」
sentences.literal: Shamelessly grasping without scruple, he is not fit to hold office."
sentences.idiomatic: He is shameless and unfit for office."

sentences.chinese: 咸坐免。
sentences.literal: Xian sat guilty and was dismissed.
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Xian was cashiered.

sentences.chinese: 頃之，紅陽侯立舉咸方正，為光祿大夫給事中，方進復奏免之。
sentences.literal: Shortly thereafter Marquis of Hongyang Wang Li recommended Xian as upright and square, and he became Grand Palace Grandee and Palace Attendant; Fangjin again memorialized and dismissed him.
sentences.idiomatic: Wang Li of Hongyang soon nominated him as "upright"; he won back a palace post until Zhai struck him down again.

sentences.chinese: 後數年，立有罪就國，方進奏歸咸故郡，以憂死。
sentences.literal: Several years later Li committed a crime and went to his fief; Fangjin memorialized to return Xian to his old commandery, where he died of grief.
sentences.idiomatic: When Wang Li later fell, Zhai had Chen Xian packed off to his home commandery, where he died of shame.

sentences.chinese: 鄭弘字稚卿，泰山剛人也。
sentences.literal: Zheng Hong, courtesy name Zhiqing, was a man of Gang in Taishan.
sentences.idiomatic: Zheng Hong, courtesy Zhiqing, came from Gang in Taishan.

sentences.chinese: 兄昌字次卿，亦好學，皆明經，通法律政事。
sentences.literal: His elder brother Chang, courtesy name Ciqing, also loved learning; both were clear in the classics and versed in statutes, law, and government affairs.
sentences.idiomatic: His elder brother Zheng Chang, courtesy Ciqing, loved books as well; both mastered the classics and the code.

sentences.chinese: 次卿為太原、涿郡太守，弘為南陽太守，皆著治跡，條教法度，為後所述。
sentences.literal: Ciqing was Guardian of Taiyuan and Zhuo commanderies, Hong was Guardian of Nanyang; both showed traces of good order, regulations and models, and were later recounted.
sentences.idiomatic: Ciqing governed Taiyuan and Zhuo, Hong governed Nanyang—each left a clean record and statutes others copied.

sentences.chinese: 次卿用刑罰深，不如弘平，遷淮陽相，以高第入為右扶風，京師稱之。
sentences.literal: Ciqing used punishments deeply, not as even as Hong; he was transferred Chancellor of Huaiyang, entered with highest rank as Youfufeng, and was praised in the capital.
sentences.idiomatic: Ciqing ruled with a heavy hand; Hong was the fairer judge. Ciqing moved to chancellor of Huaiyang, then entered as Youfufeng with top marks and won the capital's praise.

sentences.chinese: 代韋玄成為御史大夫。
sentences.literal: He succeeded Wei Xuancheng as Imperial Counselor.
sentences.idiomatic: He replaced Wei Xuancheng as imperial counselor.
